blocks.content: Callaway Park had a baseball diamond that nobody used for actual baseball in summer, which made it fair territory for other things.
The other kids were already there — Tommy Frazier, the Lester twins, and Anna, a girl visiting her grandparents for the summer who had been trying to figure out their game for a week.
They played until the sun started to angle differently — that particular shift in the afternoon light that doesn't mean evening, only that the day has been going long enough to consider it.
Kit's team was losing by three under Daisy's scoring system, which Daisy maintained was not her fault.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You can't switch teams in the middle.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Yes. Including the one about switching.
Tommy Frazier, watching this, said: "She's got you, man."
He said it like it was a joke. Daisy looked at the ground for one second before she looked back at the game.
